comments.author: example-reader
comments.text: 'The wrong assumption about an agent' is the point. Tool-configuration assumptions are so defaulted-to that it takes a conversation like this to surface them.

comments.author: example-reader
comments.text: Specification-for-humans vs. specification-for-agents is a clean cut. A lot of existing docs meant 'for agents' would be better as conversation transcripts; the information is often pre-digestion the agent doesn't need.

comments.author: example-reader
comments.text: The closing sentence — 'pre-build to avoid friction that wasn't coming' — generalizes past Claude. A lot of over-configured software is exactly this.
